problem: The area of a circle is $49\pi$ square units. What is the radius of the circle, in units?

steps.content: To find the radius of the circle, I'll use the formula for the area of a circle which is $A=\pi r^2$.
steps.text: <solve> To find the radius of the circle, I'll use the formula for the area of a circle which is $A=\pi r^2$.</solve>

steps.content: I know the area of the circle is $49\pi$ square units. So, I'll plug this into the area formula.
steps.text: <solve> I know the area of the circle is $49\pi$ square units. So, I'll plug this into the area formula.</solve>

steps.content: Using the area formula $A=\pi r^2$, and knowing the area $A=49\pi$, I can set up the equation $49\pi=\pi r^2$.

<solve> After dividing both sides by $\pi$, the equation becomes $49=r^2$.</solve>

steps.content: Taking the square root of both sides, I get $\sqrt{49}=\sqrt{r^2}$.
